text.title: Feedback Management Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Feedback Management. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: All inbound feedback channels route into one capture inbox.
rule.rationale: All inbound feedback channels route into one capture inbox.

rule.statement: Triage runs daily; SLA for first-touch ≤24h.
rule.rationale: Triage runs daily; SLA for first-touch ≤24h.

rule.statement: Each item tagged with theme + severity within triage.
rule.rationale: Each item tagged with theme + severity within triage.

rule.statement: Submitter receives a response within SLA — even 'noted, no action'.
rule.rationale: Submitter receives a response within SLA — even 'noted, no action'.

rule.statement: Themes hitting volume threshold escalate into discovery / roadmap.
rule.rationale: Themes hitting volume threshold escalate into discovery / roadmap.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-feedback-management.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
